name: Paracetamol
uses: Used to treat mild to moderate pain and to reduce fever.
warnings: Do not exceed 4000mg per day. Taking too much can cause severe liver damage.
sideEffects: Nausea, stomach pain, loss of appetite, itching, rash.

name: Amoxicillin
dosage: 250mg to 500mg every 8 hours, or 500mg to 875mg every 12 hours.
uses: Used to treat a wide variety of bacterial infections.
warnings: Do not use if you are allergic to penicillin antibiotics. Finish the entire course of medication.

name: Ibuprofen
dosage: 200mg to 400mg every 4 to 6 hours as needed.
uses: Used to reduce fever and treat pain or inflammation.
warnings: Can increase your risk of fatal heart attack or stroke. Do not use just before or after heart bypass surgery.
sideEffects: Upset stomach, mild heartburn, nausea, vomiting.

name: Amlodipine
uses: Used to treat high blood pressure and chest pain (angina).
warnings: May cause swelling of ankles, feet, or hands. Do not stop taking suddenly.

name: Levothyroxine
dosage: 50mcg to 200mcg once daily, usually in the morning on an empty stomach.
uses: Used to treat an underactive thyroid (hypothyroidism).
warnings: Do not use for weight loss. Requires regular blood tests to adjust dosage.
sideEffects: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, increased appetite, sweating, insomnia.

name: Metformin
dosage: 500mg to 2000mg daily, usually split into 2 or 3 doses.
uses: Used to improve blood sugar control in people with type 2 diabetes.
warnings: Risk of lactic acidosis (rare but serious). Do not drink alcohol while taking.
sideEffects: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, metallic taste in mouth, abdominal pain.

name: Atorvastatin
uses: Used to lower cholesterol and triglycerides in the blood.
warnings: Do not take with grapefruit juice. May cause muscle pain or weakness.

name: Omeprazole
uses: Used to reduce stomach acid for heartburn and GERD.
warnings: Long-term use may reduce calcium absorption. Take on an empty stomach.

name: Prednisone
dosage: Varies widely based on condition (5mg to 60mg daily or more).
uses: Used as an anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressant for many conditions.
warnings: Do not stop abruptly. May increase blood sugar and weaken bones with long-term use.
sideEffects: Increased appetite, weight gain, mood changes, trouble sleeping, indigestion.

name: Albuterol
dosage: 1 to 2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed.
uses: Used as a rescue inhaler for asthma attacks.
warnings: Do not overuse. Contact doctor if you need it more often than prescribed.

name: Aspirin
dosage: 325 mg to 650 mg every 4 hours as needed for pain or fever; 81 mg daily for heart protection.
uses: Reduces pain, fever, and inflammation. Also used in low doses to prevent blood clots and heart attacks.
warnings: Do not give to children or teenagers with viral infections (risk of Reye's syndrome). Can increase risk of bleeding. Do not take with alcohol.
sideEffects: Stomach upset, heartburn, nausea, easy bruising, ringing in the ears (tinnitus).

name: Lisinopril
uses: Treats high blood pressure and heart failure. Also used after a heart attack.
warnings: Can cause a dry cough. May cause dizziness, especially when standing up. Do not use during pregnancy.
sideEffects: Dizziness, headache, fatigue, cough, diarrhea, low blood pressure.

name: Simvastatin
dosage: 5 mg to 40 mg once daily in the evening.
uses: Lowers high cholesterol levels to reduce the risk of heart disease and stroke.
warnings: Can cause muscle pain or weakness (rhabdomyolysis). Do not take with grapefruit or grapefruit juice.
sideEffects: Muscle pain, headache, nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain.

name: Amoxicillin/Clavulanate
uses: Treats bacterial infections, including sinus infections, pneumonia, and ear infections.
warnings: Tell your doctor if you have had a reaction to penicillin or cephalosporins. Can cause severe diarrhea.
sideEffects: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, rash, yeast infections.

name: Hydrochlorothiazide
uses: Treats high blood pressure and fluid retention (edema).
warnings: Can increase potassium levels (monitor blood tests). May increase blood sugar and uric acid levels.
sideEffects: Dizziness, lightheadedness, frequent urination, muscle cramps, nausea.

name: Sertraline
uses: Treats depression, anxiety, OCD, and PTSD.
warnings: May increase suicidal thoughts, especially in young adults. Do not stop taking abruptly. Avoid alcohol.
sideEffects: Nausea, diarrhea, insomnia, sexual dysfunction, headache, dizziness.

name: Gabapentin
dosage: 300 mg to 600 mg three times daily for nerve pain; dosage varies for seizures.
uses: Treats nerve pain (neuropathy), restless legs syndrome, and seizures.
warnings: May cause drowsiness and dizziness. Avoid driving until you know how it affects you. Can cause withdrawal symptoms if stopped suddenly.
sideEffects: Drowsiness, dizziness, fatigue, ataxia (loss of coordination), weight gain.

name: Furosemide
dosage: 20 mg to 80 mg once or twice daily.
uses: Treats edema (fluid retention) associated with heart failure, liver disease, and kidney disease.
warnings: Can cause severe dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Monitor potassium levels closely.
sideEffects: Dizziness, dehydration, frequent urination, low potassium, muscle weakness, low blood pressure.

name: Montelukast
dosage: 10 mg once daily in the evening.
uses: Prevents asthma attacks and treats allergy symptoms.
warnings: Do not use for sudden asthma attacks. May cause mood or behavioral changes (rare).
sideEffects: Headache, upper respiratory tract infection, abdominal pain, dizziness.

name: Warfarin
dosage: Varies widely, monitored by INR testing.
uses: Prevents blood clots in conditions like atrial fibrillation and deep vein thrombosis.
warnings: Can cause severe bleeding. Requires regular INR blood tests. Many drug and food interactions (e.g., Vitamin K).
sideEffects: Bleeding, bruising, nausea, diarrhea, hair loss.

name: Dolo 650
uses: Used to reduce fever and relieve mild to moderate pain.
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose. Overdose may cause liver damage.

name: Crocin Advance
uses: Used for fever, headache, body pain.
warnings: Avoid excessive dosage to prevent liver damage.

name: Augmentin 625
dosage: One tablet every 8 to 12 hours as prescribed.
uses: Treats bacterial infections such as respiratory, urinary, and skin infections.
warnings: Complete full antibiotic course. Avoid if allergic to penicillin.

name: Azithral 500
uses: Used to treat bacterial infections including throat, chest, and skin infections.
warnings: Complete full course. May affect heart rhythm in some patients.

name: Combiflam
dosage: One tablet every 6 to 8 hours as needed.
uses: Used for fever, inflammation, and pain relief.
warnings: Avoid prolonged use. Can cause stomach ulcers or kidney issues.

name: Pantocid
uses: Used to treat acidity, GERD, and stomach ulcers.
warnings: Long-term use may reduce vitamin B12 levels.

name: Cetcip
uses: Used to treat allergies, sneezing, and itching.
warnings: May cause drowsiness. Avoid driving after use.

name: Shelcal 500
uses: Used for calcium deficiency and bone health.
warnings: Do not exceed dosage unless prescribed.

name: Glycomet
uses: Used to control blood sugar in type 2 diabetes.
warnings: Monitor kidney function regularly.

name: Telma 40
uses: Used to treat high blood pressure.
warnings: Monitor blood pressure regularly.

name: Amlokind
uses: Used for hypertension and chest pain.
warnings: May cause swelling in feet or ankles.

name: ORS Sachet
dosage: Mix in water and consume as directed.
uses: Used to prevent dehydration due to diarrhea or vomiting.
warnings: Use clean water only.

name: Volini Gel
uses: Used for muscle pain, joint pain, and sprains.
warnings: For external use only.

name: Benadryl Cough Syrup
uses: Used for cough and cold symptoms.
warnings: May cause drowsiness.

name: Ecosprin 75
uses: Used to prevent blood clots and heart attacks.
warnings: May increase bleeding risk.

name: Zifi 200
uses: Used to treat bacterial infections such as respiratory tract, urinary tract, and ear infections.
warnings: Complete full course. Avoid if allergic to cephalosporin antibiotics.

name: Sinarest
uses: Used for cold, nasal congestion, fever, and headache.
warnings: May cause drowsiness. Avoid alcohol.

name: Meftal-Spas
dosage: One tablet as prescribed during pain episodes.
uses: Used for abdominal cramps, menstrual pain, and spasms.
warnings: Avoid prolonged use. May irritate stomach.

name: Dexorange
genericName: Ferric Ammonium Citrate + Folic Acid + Vitamin B12
uses: Used to treat iron deficiency anemia.
warnings: Keep away from children. Iron overdose can be dangerous.

name: Neurobion Forte
uses: Used for vitamin B deficiency and nerve health.
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dosage.

name: Livogen
uses: Used for iron deficiency and anemia.
warnings: May cause constipation.

name: Rantac 150
uses: Used to reduce stomach acid, acidity, and ulcers.
warnings: Consult doctor for long-term use.

name: Cofsils Lozenges
uses: Used for sore throat and cough relief.
warnings: Do not exceed recommended use.

name: Digene
uses: Used for acidity, indigestion, and heartburn.
warnings: Avoid excessive use in kidney disease.

name: Supradyn
uses: Used to prevent vitamin deficiencies and improve energy.
warnings: Avoid overdose of vitamins.

name: Becosules
uses: Used for vitamin deficiencies and mouth ulcers.
warnings: Use only recommended dosage.

name: Clavam 625
uses: Used for bacterial infections.
warnings: Complete full antibiotic course.

name: Calpol 650
uses: Used for fever and body pain.
warnings: Avoid overdose.

name: Moxikind-CV
uses: Used for bacterial infections.
warnings: Complete course fully.

name: Allegra 120
uses: Used for allergies, sneezing, and hives.
warnings: Avoid fruit juice close to dosage time.
